title: Knowledge Base Node
description: A single concept in the knowledge base. The minimal unit of learning. Battle-tested in the China 1-6 curriculum project.

id.description: Unique ID. Format: <subject>.g<grade>.<slug>. Lowercase, hyphens OK in slug.

subject.description: Subject domain. Customize this enum for your domain.

grade.description: Level within the subject. For curricula, this is grade (1-6 primary, 7-9 junior, 10-12 senior). For other domains, adapt.

title.description: Human-readable name in the user's language. 2-50 chars.

kind.description: concept = a knowledge point; lesson = a teaching unit that may cover multiple concepts; exercise = a problem (usually in exercises/, not nodes/); standard = a reference to an official standard entry

description.description: Markdown body explaining the concept. 30-800 chars. For 1-6 grade, 50-150 字 works well.

prerequisites.description: IDs of concepts that must be learned before this one. Use IDs, not titles.

part_of.description: ID of the parent concept (e.g., 'math.g3.fraction' is part of 'math.g3.number.fraction'). Use sparingly; edges.json is the canonical place for relationships.

related.description: IDs of related concepts (not strictly prerequisite, but useful to know together).

difficulty.description: 1 = easiest, 5 = hardest. For 1-6 grade primary, 1-3 covers everything.

cognitive_level.description: Bloom-style cognitive level. Adapt enum to your locale. 了解/理解/掌握/应用 = remember/understand/master/apply

standard_ref.description: Reference to the official standard. E.g., 'JYB-PE-2022-math-3-04' for China 2022 primary math grade 3 code 04.

textbook_versions.description: Which textbook versions cover this concept. E.g., ['人教版', '北师大版'].

examples.description: Inline worked examples. Optional but valuable for content generation.
